name: The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — circular from Castro de Alcañices
name_original: La Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — circular desde Castro de Alcañices
region_intro: Hiking trails around Fonfría traverse a landscape characterized by its distinct mountain terrain, traditional stone architecture, and significant natural features. The region includes challenging ascents through areas like the O Cebreiro pass and the Sierra de Ancares, offering dramatic views from elevated points such as the Collada de los Ortigales (2,056 meters). Shaded forest paths are common, and the area is recognized as a protected natural zone. A natural water spring, the origin of the town's name, is a notable feature. Best hiking trails around Fonfría The most popular hiking route is The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — circular from Castro de Alcañices, a 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and riverside sections. Another top favourite among local hikers is Via verde del rio Angueira — circular desde Alcanices, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail follows the Angueira river, offering scenic views and a tranquil environment. Local hikers also love the La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail leading through natural landscapes to a waterfall, often completed in about 57 minutes. Hiking around Fonfría is defined by mountain landscapes, shaded forest paths, and dramatic river canyons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Fonfría are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from several reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Fonfría's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fonfría?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; in the Fonfría region, offering a variety of experiences. These include 4 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Fonfría?
A: Yes, Fonfría offers several easy routes suitable for families. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 km long and takes about 57 minutes to complete, leading through natural landscapes to a waterfall. Another easy route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.4 km path.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Fonfría area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Fonfría are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025144528/the-rivera-de-los-molinos-de-brandilanes-circular-from-castro-de-alcanices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Rivera de los Molinos de Brandilanes — circular from Castro de Alcañices&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.6 km route with varied terrain and riverside sections, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026773591/via-verde-del-rio-angueira-circular-desde-alcanices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via verde del rio Angueira — circular desde Alcanices&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.1 km path following the Angueira river.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes around Fonfría?
A: Yes, you can hike to waterfalls in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy trail specifically leading to a waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadozos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight in the area.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Fonfría?
A: Fonfría is characterized by stunning mountain landscapes, traditional stone architecture, and significant natural features. You'll find shaded forest paths, dramatic river canyons, and impressive elevations offering spectacular views from points like the Collada de los Ortigales (2,056 meters). The area is also home to a natural water spring, which is the origin of the town's name.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alba Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or visit various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the Douro Canyon, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São João das Arribas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/787174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Douro Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7870126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrilhouço Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3409877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requejo Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another interesting structure.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fonfría?
A: The routes in Fonfría are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.7 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from riverside sections to shaded forest paths, and the tranquil environment found on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026773591/via-verde-del-rio-angueira-circular-desde-alcanices" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via verde del rio Angueira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Fonfría part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Fonfría is a significant point on the historic &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago&lt;/strong&gt;, specifically the challenging Camino Francés. Pilgrims and hikers passing through the village can experience traditional elements, including an outdoor washing area still used by residents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fonfría?
A: While hiking is possible year-round, the best time to visit Fonfría for hiking is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter can bring colder temperatures and snow to higher mountain passes like O Cebreiro.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Douro River or Canyon?
A: Yes, the nearby International Douro Natural Park features the majestic Douro Canyon. Trails in this area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283120270/rio-duero-desde-pino-del-oro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Duero desde Pino del Oro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, offer opportunities to experience the river vistas. Viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São João das Arribas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/787174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Douro Canyon&lt;/a&gt; provide panoramic views of the rugged geological formations.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in Fonfría?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across the region's trails. While some easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt; have a modest gain of around 63 meters, more challenging routes can involve significant ascents through areas like the O Cebreiro pass and the Sierra de Ancares, reaching elevated points over 2,000 meters.
tips:
The Molino de los Carbajos is an old mill located in the Aliste region, near Fonfría, in the province of Zamora. This mill is a popular point of interest for hiking and nature lovers. It is located on a marked trail that runs from Brandilanes to Castro, following the white and yellow signs.
The Molino de los Carbajos is perfect for a short excursion and to enjoy the natural beauty of Aliste.

The Molino de los Tejeros is located on the hiking route that goes from Castro de Alcañices to Brandilanes, in the Aliste region, Zamora. This route, known as the Rivera de los Molinos, is famous for its natural beauty and its ethnological heritage, since along the route you can see several old water mills.

The La Ribera Stream Bridge in Fonfría is a picturesque spot perfect for hiking and nature lovers. It's a stone bridge that allows you to cross the La Ribera Stream.
The area offers several scenic trails to enjoy the nature and scenery.

waypoints.name: Stone Bridge over the La Ribera Stream
